p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future Margery de Vescy sends greeting.

to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and the monks in that place serving God for the salvation of my own soul and all my ancestors' and successors' (souls), one ploughgate of land in the territory of Lillliesleaf, that is, the one which Walter of Ryedale son of Patrick gave with all easements and liberties and by the same boundaries which are contained in the charter of the aforesaid Walter. Wherefore I will and grant that the aforesaid monks should have and hold the aforesaid land in free, pure and everlasting alms, with all easements and liberties pertaining to the same, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably, without every custom and secular exaction, just as they hold and possess any alms freely, quietly, fully and honourably in the kingdom of Scotland, reserving to myself and my heirs the service pertaining to the same land from the aforesaid Walter de Ryedale an his heirs. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant all things aforesaid to the aforementioned monks, that they may have, hold, possess and use them by all means which seem best and convenient to them for ever.
With these witnesses, Roger de Merlay, Adam of Polwarth, Alexander of Seton, Bernard of Howden, William de Vescy, Master William of Ednam, Ralph of Ryedale, Robert of Barnoldby, and many others.
